ea_prompt.parent_head_capture_note: Captured at SDO authoring time. EA must re-capture at the start of execution via `git rev-parse HEAD` on BlarAI and substitute the live value into the comprehension comment. EA-4 is BlarAI-only writes; the devplatform side is not touched at all (no read, no write). The investigation reads BlarAI test/config files only.

attachment.reason: Authoritative scope; SS5.1 EA-4 deliverable specification (report sections SS1-SS6, methodology candidates, fail-closed surface check); SS5.2 non-goals (no production source edits, no test-file edits, no pyproject.toml / conftest.py edits, no ADR/DEC amendments, no CLAUDE.md edits); SS5.3 EA-4 boundary (methodology at EA's discretion within scope; fail-closed weakening = CRITICAL stop-and-escalate; recommendation scope is SS6 advisory only -- EA-4 does NOT directly edit CLAUDE.md Active State); SS7 sequencing (strictly after EA-3 merge to BlarAI main).

Sprint 11 continuation; lessons L-12 (verbatim comprehension recitation), L-13 (parent_head capture), L-15 (working-set declaration), L-22 (mature-not-minimal floors), L-25 (evidence-first: bisect log / commit enumeration / marker chain trace are captured verbatim, not paraphrased).

Sprint 8 SWAGR SS8 baseline-strings section -- the post-Sprint-8-EA-5 baseline ~981 passed, 22 skipped that EA-4 takes as the lower bound of the drift window. Confirm exact filename via Glob at EA start (post-EA-3 the file path is stable).

Sprint 10 SWAGR SS8.1 baseline-strings section -- the post-Sprint-10 baseline 1001 passed, 2 skipped that EA-4 takes as the upper bound of the drift window. Confirm exact filename via Glob at EA start.
Sprint 10 SCR -- the SCR commit hash anchors the upper-bound test-run reference. The exact SCR commit is the upper-bound boundary for the bisect / commit-enumeration window.

Project-wide pytest configuration + marker definitions. Read end-to-end to identify the current marker taxonomy and any [tool.pytest.ini_options] block; do NOT edit. The marker taxonomy is the universe within which the 20 test flips happened.

Project-root conftest.py if present. Read end-to-end -- contains fixtures / autouse hooks / collection hooks that could change which tests get collected and run between Sprint 8 close and Sprint 10 SCR. Do NOT edit.

Marker taxonomy reference and baseline-management policy. Read end-to-end to ground SS5/SS6 of the investigation report in the project's stated test policy. EXPLICITLY OUT-OF-SCOPE FOR EDITS per SDV SS5.2; reference and cite only.

The active state block at line ~93 carries the current SDV-anchored test baseline string (~981 passed, 22 skipped). Read for context -- this is the string EA-4's SS6 recommendation targets, but EA-4 does NOT edit it (SS6 is an advisory recommendation; the actual CLAUDE.md amendment is EA-2 territory or a Sprint 12+ follow-up).
Q1-1 per-file ledger schema + frontmatter spec (formalized by EA-1's DEC-17). The EA-4 ledger entry follows this schema exactly.

ea_prompt.pre_flight:
Sprint 11 EA-4 is the FOURTH EA in Sprint 11. EA-1 (DEC bundle), EA-2 (Active State refresh procedure), and EA-3 (SWAGR cross-repo template + SDV pointer fix) are all landed on BlarAI main (EA-1 and EA-2 also on devplatform main; EA-3 BlarAI-only). EA-4 is the investigation-only EA: it READS extensively (test files, config, git history, Sprint 8/10 SWAGRs), but WRITES exactly TWO BlarAI files (the investigation report + one ledger entry) on a feature branch. NO test edits. NO production source edits. NO config edits. NO doctrine edits. NO devplatform writes.
Working-set verification. Before any reads or branch creation, Test-Path each of: - C:\Users\mrbla\BlarAI\docs\sprints\sprint_11\ (must return True -- report destination directory) - C:\Users\mrbla\BlarAI\docs\sprints\sprint_11\test_baseline_drift_investigation.md (MUST return False -- report does not yet exist) - C:\Users\mrbla\BlarAI\docs\ledger\ (must return True -- ledger destination) - C:\Users\mrbla\BlarAI\pyproject.toml (must return True -- pytest config source) - C:\Users\mrbla\BlarAI\conftest.py (capture True/False -- some repos have it project-root, some per-package; record verbatim either way) - C:\Users\mrbla\BlarAI\docs\TEST_GOVERNANCE.md (must return True -- policy reference)
Confirm EA-3 is landed on BlarAI main: cd C:\Users\mrbla\BlarAI git log --oneline main | Select-String 'Sprint 11 EA-3|EA-3 SWAGR cross-repo' | Select-Object -First 4 Expected: at least one matching line; EA-3 merge commit visible at HEAD or near HEAD.
Conflict check: no prior EA-4 staged or finalized prompt should exist outside this staging file; no prior ledger entry with the EA-4 slug; no prior investigation report: Get-ChildItem C:\Users\mrbla\BlarAI\docs\scheduled\ea_queue\ -Filter '*_ea4_*.xml' Get-ChildItem C:\Users\mrbla\BlarAI\docs\ledger\ -Filter '*sprint11_ea4*.md' Test-Path C:\Users\mrbla\BlarAI\docs\sprints\sprint_11\test_baseline_drift_investigation.md Expected: empty / empty / False. Any conflict -> STOP and escalate; do NOT overwrite.
Boundary refs for the drift window. EA-4 captures the EXACT refs at execution time (these were approximate at SDO authoring time): - Lower bound: Sprint 8 EA-5 merge commit (post-Sprint-8 close). Identify by: git log --oneline main | Select-String 'Sprint 8 EA-5|sprint:8.*EA-5' | Select-Object -First 4 Capture verbatim the merge or EA-5 completion commit hash; use as `` throughout. SDO's approximate reference at authoring time: ~06ea25e (verify; the EA captures the live value). - Upper bound: Sprint 10 SCR commit. Identify by: git log --oneline main | Select-String 'Sprint 10 SCR|sprint:10.*SCR|Strategic_Completion_Report_Sprint_10' | Select-Object -First 4 Capture verbatim the SCR commit hash; use as `` throughout. SDO's approximate reference at authoring time: ~90db41f (verify).
Fleet-Pause SOP (per feedback_pause_fleet_for_git_work). PAUSE the fleet before any branch checkout or pytest run, because EA-4 will execute pytest at multiple commit points and that's expensive:
$env:PYTHONPATH = "C:\Users\mrbla\devplatform"; python -c "from tools.autonomy_budget import state; state.pause_fleet('Sprint 11 EA-4 test-baseline drift investigation', updated_by='ea_code', path='C:/Users/mrbla/devplatform/tools/autonomy_budget/state.json')" cd C:\Users\mrbla\BlarAI; git commit --allow-empty -m "chore(ops): pause fleet -- Sprint 11 EA-4 test-baseline drift investigation"
Resume LAST, just before posting the completion comment: $env:PYTHONPATH = "C:\Users\mrbla\devplatform"; python -c "from tools.autonomy_budget import state; state.resume_fleet(updated_by='ea_code', path='C:/Users/mrbla/devplatform/tools/autonomy_budget/state.json')" cd C:\Users\mrbla\BlarAI; git commit --allow-empty -m "chore(ops): unpause fleet -- Sprint 11 EA-4 done"
API-NAME GOTCHA: function is `resume_fleet`, NOT `unpause_fleet`.
Serial execution context. EA-1, EA-2, EA-3 are landed. EA-5 (doctrine + doc-hygiene cleanup) is gated on EA-4 merge per SDV SS7. Do NOT touch any EA-5 working-set path (see negative_constraints).
ea_prompt.comprehension_gate:
BEFORE any reads beyond the required_attachments, any branch creation, any pytest run, post a comprehension comment to Vikunja tracking task #410 prefixed `[agent:ea_code][phase:comprehension]` containing ALL of:
1. Milestone objective in your own words: what investigation EA-4 is conducting; why now (CLAUDE.md SDV-anchored baseline ~981 passed / 22 skipped vs Sprint 10 SWAGR SS8.1 baseline 1001 passed / 2 skipped -- a +20 pass / -20 skip drift that arose between sprints, outside any sprint's stated working set, so no prior SWAGR audited it); who consumes the report downstream (the LA, the Sprint Auditor authoring Sprint 11 SWAGR, future sprints needing a refreshed baseline string); what category of outcome is in scope (drift category benign / suspicious / regression) and what is NOT in scope (direct CLAUDE.md edit, pyproject.toml edit, test edits -- all advisory in SS6 only).
2. Per-deliverable work-item summary. WI-1 through WI-8 -- one sentence each in your own words.
3. Files to create/modify (absolute paths). EXACTLY two files written, both BlarAI-side: - C:\Users\mrbla\BlarAI\docs\sprints\sprint_11\test_baseline_drift_investigation.md (new; investigation report; >= 80 lines content) - C:\Users\mrbla\BlarAI\docs\ledger\TS_sprint11_ea4_test-baseline-drift.md (new; Q1-1 ledger entry; TS = UTC YYYYMMDD_HHMMSS captured at EA start)
NO other files are edited. No test code (test files are READ). No production source. No pyproject.toml. No conftest.py. No ADR. No DEC document. No CLAUDE.md. No active_tasks.yaml. No wake template. No SWAGR / SCR / SDV template. No devplatform file (no read, no write).
4. Source files to read (read-only). All required_attachments above, plus: - Selected test files under shared/, services/, launcher/ identified at WI-3 as candidates for the 20 newly-running tests (marker inspection, assertion-shape inspection); each identified file READ-ONLY. - Git history between `` and `` -- specifically commits touching pyproject.toml, conftest.py, tests/, shared/conftest.py, services/*/conftest.py, launcher/conftest.py, and any project-wide collection-hook surface.
5. EXACT report structure -- recite VERBATIM by section heading:
File: docs/sprints/sprint_11/test_baseline_drift_investigation.md
Frontmatter (optional but recommended): --- report_type: test_baseline_drift_investigation sprint_id: 11 ea_number: 4 lower_bound_commit: upper_bound_commit: drift_summary: "+20 pass / -20 skip (baseline ~981/22 -> 1001/2)" drift_category: (set at EA close) ---
SS1 Methodology (>= 8 lines): - State methodology choice: (a) git-bisect, OR (b) commit-log enumeration of pyproject.toml/conftest.py/tests/ touches, OR (c) marker-resolution analysis at HEAD with retroactive verification, OR (d) hybrid. - Rationale: why this methodology over the alternatives. - Acceptance criteria for the methodology to be considered conclusive (e.g., "if bisect converges on a single commit, that commit is the root cause; if multiple commits each contribute partial drift, enumerate all").
SS2 Bisect log / commit enumeration (>= 10 lines): - Verbatim git log / git bisect output (the commits inspected or bisected, with one-line abbreviated descriptors). - For each commit, the pass/skip count at that commit (if methodology (a) or (b)). For methodology (c) skip this section and replace with the marker-resolution diff table. - All hashes captured verbatim; no paraphrasing.
SS3 Identified test names that flipped from skip -> pass (>= 20 lines OR a table with >= 20 rows): - For each of the 20 newly-running tests (identified by name): a) fully-qualified test path (path/to/test_file.py::TestClass::test_method); b) the marker / skip-condition at the lower bound; c) the marker / skip-condition at the upper bound; d) the commit (or commit set) responsible for the change; e) classification: skip-condition-relaxed, marker-renamed, fixture-newly-available, environment-resolved, dependency-resolved, or other (specify). - If the count is not exactly 20 (e.g., +18 pass / -22 skip, +22 pass / -18 skip), enumerate the actual count and explain the asymmetry.
SS4 Fail-closed surface verification (FORMAL TABLE): - For every newly-running test under services/policy_agent/, services/assistant_orchestrator/, shared/crypto/, shared/ipc/, services/semantic_router/, OR any test asserting on `last_failure["code"]`, `Sensitivity.UNCLASSIFIED`, PGOV thresholds, or vsock topology: produce a row in the table with columns: | Test name | File:line | Pre-drift assertion shape | Post-drift assertion shape | Weakening detected? (Y/N) | Severity if Y | - Pre/post assertion shape: capture the EXACT lines from the test (file:line cite + verbatim assertion expression). - If table is empty (no fail-closed-surface tests in the drift set), state explicitly: "Empty -- no newly-running test touches a fail-closed surface. Verified by enumerating all 20 test names against the listed module / assertion- pattern surfaces." - **CRITICAL EXIT condition**: if ANY weakening detected (Pre asserts stricter than Post), STOP IMMEDIATELY -- do NOT continue with SS5/SS6, do NOT commit, do NOT merge. File the finding via [agent:ea_code][phase:completion] on task #410 with CRITICAL severity callout, apply Gate:Pending-Human (label id 11), and wait for LA direction. The partial investigation file may be staged on the feature branch but not merged.
SS5 Conclusion -- drift category (>= 6 lines): - State the drift category: benign / suspicious / regression. - benign: skip conditions resolved by environment / fixture availability; no fail-closed weakening; net test coverage grew. - suspicious: drift cause unclear or partially attributable to fixture / env changes that may not be reproducible. - regression: a previously-skipped fail-closed test now passes but the assertion shape has been weakened; CRITICAL surfaces above already triggers stop-and-escalate (so "regression" here means assertion-shape-stable but surfaces-of-concern surfaced; this is a narrower bucket). - Reasoning: cite SS3 + SS4 evidence directly.
SS6 Recommendation for Sprint 12+ (>= 10 lines): - Recommended replacement baseline string for CLAUDE.md Active State (e.g., "Test baseline (post-Sprint-10 SCR): 1001 passed, 2 skipped on `pytest shared/ services/ launcher/`"). - Recommended timing: at next SDV authoring (Sprint 12 kickoff) per EA-2's Active State refresh procedure, OR inline now as a Stage 6.7.5 doc-hygiene item -- EA-4 picks the recommendation within scope; document rationale. - Recommended SWAGR template / SDV / SDP / CLAUDE.md cross- reference language to prevent recurrence (e.g., "each SWAGR captures both lower-bound and upper-bound baseline; any cross-sprint drift triggers a single-EA investigation ticket"). Cite the cross-reference target by absolute path. - This section is ADVISORY ONLY. EA-4 does NOT directly edit CLAUDE.md, SDV templates, SWAGR templates, or wake templates.
Length floor for the report: >= 80 lines of substantive content (excluding frontmatter and section heading lines). Mature-not- minimal: SS3 enumerates real test names found by real inspection (no `` rows); SS4 cites real file:line references; SS5 cites SS3/SS4 evidence directly; SS6 produces a concrete baseline string and concrete cross-reference text.
File: docs/ledger/TS_sprint11_ea4_test-baseline-drift.md Q1-1 schema per docs/ledger/README.md (formalized by EA-1's DEC-17). Frontmatter (ledger_id, date, sprint_id=11, entry_type=EA, predecessor, branch= feature/p5-task11-ea4-test-baseline-drift-investigation, merge_commit=null, disposition=COMPLETE) -> SSSummary -> SSDeliverables -> SSFiles Changed -> SSQuality Gate (verbatim Test-Path + boundary-hash capture + pytest run-count outputs) -> SSDEC References (link to DEC-15 sprint-lifecycle, DEC-17 ledger format, EA-2 Active State refresh procedure). Length floor: >= 35 lines content (excluding frontmatter).
6. ORACLE expectation (verbatim, executed at EA close after the BlarAI commit lands):
cd C:\Users\mrbla\BlarAI git diff main...feature/p5-task11-ea4-test-baseline-drift-investigation --name-only Expected (sorted, exactly 2 files): docs/ledger/TS_sprint11_ea4_test-baseline-drift.md docs/sprints/sprint_11/test_baseline_drift_investigation.md Any other path -> ORACLE violation; STOP and report.
7. Working-set declaration (L-15). Verbatim: "I write to exactly two absolute paths -- the investigation report at C:\Users\mrbla\BlarAI\docs\sprints\sprint_11\test_baseline_drift_investigation.md and the Q1-1 ledger entry at C:\Users\mrbla\BlarAI\docs\ledger\TS_sprint11_ea4_test-baseline-drift.md. Both are BlarAI-only. I read extensively across BlarAI test sources, pyproject.toml, conftest.py, TEST_GOVERNANCE.md, Sprint 8/10 SWAGRs, and git history. I touch NO test file for editing, NO production source, NO pyproject.toml or conftest.py edit, NO CLAUDE.md edit, NO ADR or DEC document, NO active_tasks.yaml, NO wake template, NO SWAGR/SCR/SDV template, NO devplatform file at all. If I find a fail-closed weakening or any other regression-class signal during SS4 verification, I STOP, file the finding via the completion comment with CRITICAL severity, apply Gate:Pending-Human, and wait for LA direction. I do NOT silently reconcile."
8. Mature-not-minimal acknowledgment (L-22). Verbatim: "Mature not minimal: the report is >= 80 lines of SUBSTANTIVE content grounded in real bisect log / commit enumeration / marker inspection -- no `` rows in SS3, no empty SS4 if any fail-closed-surface tests appear in the drift set, no hand-wave SS5 conclusion that bypasses SS3/SS4 evidence. The ledger entry is >= 35 lines with verbatim Quality Gate evidence (boundary-hash captures, pytest run-count outputs, Test-Path outputs). Padding to hit floors is rejected -- a focused 95-line report grounded in real evidence beats a 150-line report padded with redundant cross-references."
9. Evidence-first discipline (L-25). Verbatim: "Every claim about a commit, a test name, a marker resolution, an assertion shape, or a baseline number is grounded in an empirical observation captured verbatim in the report (commit hash, test path, file:line cite, exact assertion expression, exact pytest output line). No paraphrasing of source text. No 'approximately'. No 'roughly'. Boundary hashes are captured verbatim; if SDO's approximate authoring-time refs (~06ea25e, ~90db41f) don't resolve, the EA finds the actual refs by grep over git log and records the live refs in SS2 prose."
10. Methodology-discretion acknowledgment (Sprint-11-specific). Verbatim: "Methodology is at EA-4's discretion within the SDV SS5.3 boundary of 'a real investigation'. The three candidate methodologies (git-bisect, commit-log enumeration, marker- resolution analysis) are encoded by the SDO without pre-decision. EA-4 picks the methodology that fits the observed drift shape (a single conftest.py commit suggests commit-log enum; a slow drift across many commits suggests bisect; a sudden marker-taxonomy change suggests marker analysis). EA-4 documents the choice and rationale in SS1."
11. Fail-closed STOP-discipline acknowledgment. Verbatim: "If SS4 surfaces ANY assertion-shape weakening on a fail-closed-surface test, I IMMEDIATELY STOP the investigation, file the finding via [agent:ea_code][phase:completion] on task #410 with the text 'CRITICAL FINDING -- fail-closed assertion weakening detected at ', apply Gate:Pending-Human (label id 11), and WAIT for LA direction. I do NOT attempt to fix the weakened test (that would be a production-source edit, out of scope). I do NOT complete SS5/SS6 in this case -- those sections are left empty or marked 'PENDING-LA' in the report. I do NOT merge the feature branch."
12. No-CLAUDE.md-edit acknowledgment. Verbatim: "EA-4 produces an SS6 recommendation about the next CLAUDE.md Active State baseline string, but EA-4 does NOT edit CLAUDE.md itself. CLAUDE.md amendment is a Sprint 12+ follow-up or an EA-2-style Active State refresh run; EA-4's recommendation lives in the report only."
13. Risks and ambiguities. State any unresolved understanding before starting -- especially: (a) methodology choice and rationale; (b) whether the drift count is exactly +20 / -20 or approximate; (c) whether the bisect window crosses any commit that touches a fail-closed-surface module (which increases SS4 attention requirement); (d) whether running pytest at multiple historical commits requires environment rehydration (e.g., re-running poetry / uv install between commits) -- if yes, methodology (c) marker-analysis-at-HEAD may be more tractable than methodology (a) bisect.
After posting the comprehension comment, apply label `Gate:Pending-SDO` (id 9) on Vikunja task #410 and STOP. WAIT for SDO Phase 1a review verdict before doing any work beyond the required-attachment reads. Do NOT bundle downstream work with the comprehension comment.

milestone.objective: Conduct a real investigation into the +20 pass / -20 skip movement between Sprint 8 EA-5 close (~981 passed, 22 skipped per CLAUDE.md SDV-anchored baseline) and Sprint 10 SCR commit (1001 passed, 2 skipped per Sprint 10 SWAGR SS8.1). Produce a per-test breakdown with fail-closed surface verification, root- cause attribution, drift-category determination (benign / suspicious / regression), and Sprint 12+ baseline-string recommendation. Land one BlarAI feature-branch commit (merged via Co-Lead trusted_scope) containing the investigation report plus a Q1-1 ledger entry. Closes Sprint 10 SWAGR SS8.1 follow-up recommendation and Sprint 11 success criterion #4.
milestone.size_rationale: Real investigation potentially spanning many commits + per-test verification + fail-closed surface check + report writing. Largest variance among Sprint 11 EAs (30 min if bisect converges on a single commit; multi-hour if cause is environment-level or spans many commits). Report >= 80 lines + ledger >= 35 lines + investigation overhead. Aggregate write footprint is small (2 files), but cognitive + execution overhead is non-trivial.

work_items.wi:
WI-1: Pre-flight conflict + dependency check.
Capture parent_head: cd C:\Users\mrbla\BlarAI git rev-parse HEAD Record verbatim; substitute live value if SDO's authoring-time value (eddb302) has drifted.
Verify input file presence via Test-Path (capture each verbatim): - C:\Users\mrbla\BlarAI\docs\sprints\sprint_11\ -- must return True - C:\Users\mrbla\BlarAI\docs\ledger\ -- must return True - C:\Users\mrbla\BlarAI\pyproject.toml -- must return True - C:\Users\mrbla\BlarAI\conftest.py -- capture True/False (project may or may not have a root-level conftest) - C:\Users\mrbla\BlarAI\docs\TEST_GOVERNANCE.md -- must return True - C:\Users\mrbla\BlarAI\docs\sprints\sprint_11\test_baseline_drift_investigation.md -- MUST return False (report does not yet exist)
Verify no prior EA-4 artifact exists: Get-ChildItem C:\Users\mrbla\BlarAI\docs\scheduled\ea_queue\ -Filter '*_ea4_*.xml' -- expected: empty (or only the EA-4 staging file at staging\, which is THIS prompt) Get-ChildItem C:\Users\mrbla\BlarAI\docs\ledger\ -Filter '*sprint11_ea4*.md' -- expected: empty
Verify EA-3 landed on BlarAI main: cd C:\Users\mrbla\BlarAI git log --oneline main | Select-String 'Sprint 11 EA-3|EA-3 SWAGR cross-repo' | Select-Object -First 4 Expected: EA-3 merge commit (or completion commit) visible.
Identify boundary refs verbatim: Lower bound (Sprint 8 EA-5 close): git log --oneline main | Select-String 'Sprint 8 EA-5|sprint:8.*EA-5' | Select-Object -First 4 Pick the most-recent matching commit at or after Sprint 8 close as . Record verbatim. Upper bound (Sprint 10 SCR): git log --oneline main | Select-String 'Sprint 10 SCR|sprint:10.*SCR|Strategic_Completion_Report_Sprint_10' | Select-Object -First 4 Pick the SCR-authoring commit as . Record verbatim. If multiple SCR-related commits exist, pick the earliest -- the SCR-publication commit -- and document the choice rationale in SS2.
Capture the baseline pytest output at HEAD (the verification point for SS6's recommendation; do NOT run yet at historical commits -- that's WI-2 if methodology requires it): .venv\Scripts\pytest shared/ services/ launcher/ --tb=no -q (Or the project's canonical invocation per TEST_GOVERNANCE.md.) Capture the final summary line verbatim (e.g., "1001 passed, 2 skipped in 47.32s"). This becomes one input to SS3 (which tests are passing at HEAD vs what was skipped at ).
All Test-Path outputs, the parent_head capture, both boundary hash captures, and the HEAD pytest summary line are recorded verbatim for the ledger entry's SSQuality Gate section.

WI-2: Methodology choice + execution. The investigation methodology is at EA-4's discretion within the SDV SS5.3 boundary. Pick one of the three candidates (or a hybrid) and document the choice in SS1 of the report.
Candidate (a) -- git-bisect: Set up: git bisect start git bisect bad git bisect good At each step: .venv\Scripts\pytest shared/ services/ launcher/ --tb=no -q Record the pass/skip count. If close to upper baseline, mark "bad"; if close to lower baseline, mark "good"; iterate. Output: the single commit that flips the count to upper baseline. Record verbatim git bisect log for SS2.
Candidate (b) -- commit-log enumeration: cd C:\Users\mrbla\BlarAI git log --oneline .. -- pyproject.toml conftest.py tests/ shared/conftest.py services/*/conftest.py launcher/conftest.py Record each commit + the file(s) it touched. For each commit, check out and run pytest (or use git show to inspect the diff and determine without re-run whether the diff alters marker / skip resolution). Record per-commit outcomes for SS2.
Candidate (c) -- marker-resolution analysis at HEAD: Generate the collection diff: .venv\Scripts\pytest shared/ services/ launcher/ --collect-only -q Compare against the same invocation at (requires checking out and re-running, OR using a saved collection-output if Sprint 8 SWAGR captured one). The collection diff identifies the 20 tests that flipped from skip -> pass. For each test, inspect its @pytest.mark.skip / @pytest.mark.skipif / @pytest.mark.xfail conditional and trace through any fixture / env / marker-config change that would resolve it differently at HEAD vs .
Hybrid: methodologies may combine -- e.g., (b) to narrow to a handful of suspect commits, then (a) to bisect among them, then (c) to enumerate the specific test names at the converged commit. Document the chosen path explicitly in SS1.
During execution, if any pytest run produces an UNEXPECTED failure (a previously-passing test now failing), STOP -- this is a different drift class than +pass / -skip and may indicate a regression EA-4 was not commissioned to find. File the finding via [agent:ea_code][phase:completion] with severity callout and escalate.

WI-4: Fail-closed surface verification. The CRITICAL section.
Enumerate the 20 newly-running tests against the fail-closed- surface filter: - File path matches: services/policy_agent/, services/assistant_orchestrator/, shared/crypto/, shared/ipc/, services/semantic_router/ - OR assertion content matches: last_failure["code"], Sensitivity.UNCLASSIFIED, PGOV thresholds, vsock topology.
For each test that matches the filter (file path OR assertion content), produce a formal-table row in SS4 of the report: | Test name | File:line | Pre-drift assertion shape | Post-drift assertion shape | Weakening detected? (Y/N) | Severity if Y |
Pre-drift assertion shape: verbatim assertion-line content at (use `git show :path/to/test.py` and find the assertion). Post-drift: same at .
Weakening determination: - Pre `assert x == strict_value` -> Post `assert x is not None` = WEAKENING (Y). - Pre `assert mode is FAIL_CLOSED` -> Post `assert mode in {FAIL_CLOSED, DEGRADED}` = WEAKENING (Y). - Pre `assert resp.status == REJECT` -> Post `assert resp.status == REJECT` (unchanged) = NOT weakening (N). - New test that has no pre-drift version (skipif resolved to False so the test never ran before) but the assertion is stricter or equally strict relative to the surrounding test-suite norm = NOT weakening (N). - Severity: if Y, MEDIUM unless the assertion was directly gating policy-violation rejection or sensitive-data containment, in which case HIGH or CRITICAL.
**CRITICAL EXIT**: if ANY row has Weakening = Y at severity HIGH or CRITICAL, the investigation STOPS at this WI: - Do NOT complete SS5 or SS6 in the report. - Do NOT commit, do NOT merge. - Post completion comment with "CRITICAL FINDING -- fail-closed assertion weakening detected at ; severity ; pre-drift / post-drift assertion shapes captured in partial-report SS4". - Apply Gate:Pending-Human (label id 11) on task #410. - Wait for LA direction. The report file may be staged on the feature branch in its partial state but is NOT merged.
If the table is empty (no fail-closed-surface tests in the drift set), state explicitly in SS4: "Empty -- no newly-running test touches a fail-closed surface. Verified by enumerating all 20 test names from SS3 against the listed module paths (services/policy_agent/, services/assistant_orchestrator/, shared/crypto/, shared/ipc/, services/semantic_router/) and against assertion-pattern matches for last_failure[\"code\"], Sensitivity.UNCLASSIFIED, PGOV thresholds, vsock topology. Zero matches."

WI-5: Author SS5 + SS6 of the report (drift category + Sprint 12+ recommendation).
SS5 Conclusion -- drift category: - benign / suspicious / regression -- pick one and justify from SS3 + SS4 evidence. - Specific reasoning: cite the SS3 classification distribution (e.g., "17 of 20 tests had skip-condition-relaxed via fixture newly available in a CI/conftest change; 3 had environment-resolved via package upgrade") and the SS4 weakening-table outcome.
SS6 Recommendation for Sprint 12+: - Recommended replacement CLAUDE.md baseline string. Capture verbatim (e.g., "Test baseline (post-Sprint-10 SCR): 1001 passed, 2 skipped on `pytest shared/ services/ launcher/`"). The exact numbers come from WI-1's HEAD pytest output. - Recommended timing: at next SDV authoring (Sprint 12 kickoff) per EA-2's Active State refresh procedure, OR inline at Stage 6.7.5 doc-hygiene cleanup -- EA-4 picks, document rationale. - Recommended SWAGR template / SDV / CLAUDE.md cross-reference language to prevent recurrence. Suggest one specific change (e.g., "amend SWAGR template SS8 baseline strings subsection to require both lower-bound and upper-bound capture so cross-sprint drift surfaces in the SWAGR rather than requiring a separate investigation"). Cite the cross-reference target by absolute path.
Length floor: SS5 >= 6 lines; SS6 >= 10 lines.

WI-6: Author the BlarAI ledger entry on the feature branch. Path: C:\Users\mrbla\BlarAI\docs\ledger\TS_sprint11_ea4_test-baseline-drift.md where TS = UTC YYYYMMDD_HHMMSS captured at EA start.
Frontmatter (Q1-1 schema per docs/ledger/README.md, formalized by EA-1's DEC-17): --- ledger_id: TS_sprint11_ea4_test-baseline-drift date: YYYY-MM-DD sprint_id: 11 entry_type: EA predecessor: branch: feature/p5-task11-ea4-test-baseline-drift-investigation merge_commit: null disposition: COMPLETE ---
Body sections: SSSummary (3-5 sentences): motivation chain (Sprint 8 EA-5 baseline -> Sprint 10 SCR baseline -> +20/-20 drift surfaced in Sprint 10 SWAGR SS8.1 -> Sprint 11 EA-4 investigation); methodology choice rationale; drift category determination; Sprint 12+ recommendation pointer. SSDeliverables (bulleted, absolute paths): C:\Users\mrbla\BlarAI\docs\sprints\sprint_11\test_baseline_drift_investigation.md (with line count); this ledger entry. SSFiles Changed: single sub-table for the BlarAI commit hash + paths. SSQuality Gate: verbatim WI-1 parent_head capture + Test-Path outputs + boundary-hash captures + HEAD pytest summary line; verbatim WI-2 methodology-output excerpt (a few representative lines from the bisect log / commit enum / collection diff); verbatim WI-4 fail-closed-surface- table summary line (e.g., "0 weakening detected" or "X weakening rows at severity Y"). SSDEC References: link to DEC-15 (sprint-lifecycle), DEC-17 (Q1-1 ledger format -- landed in EA-1), and a reference to EA-2's Active State refresh procedure (C:\Users\mrbla\BlarAI\docs\runbooks\active_state_refresh.md if EA-2 landed it under that path; verify and substitute actual path at EA execution time).
Length floor: >= 35 lines content (excluding frontmatter).

WI-7: Commit BlarAI side. Create feature branch and commit: cd C:\Users\mrbla\BlarAI git checkout -b feature/p5-task11-ea4-test-baseline-drift-investigation git add docs/sprints/sprint_11/test_baseline_drift_investigation.md git add docs/ledger/TS_sprint11_ea4_test-baseline-drift.md git commit -m "[sprint:11][role:ea_code][phase:completion] EA-4 test-baseline drift investigation report + ledger"
Commit body cites: (a) the drift summary (Sprint 8 EA-5 close ~981/22 -> Sprint 10 SCR 1001/2 -> +20 pass / -20 skip); (b) the methodology chosen and one-sentence rationale; (c) the drift category determination (benign / suspicious / regression) with one-sentence justification; (d) the SS4 fail-closed-surface- verification outcome (count of rows; weakening count if any); (e) one-sentence statement that EA-4 is BlarAI-only writes with NO test / production-source / pyproject.toml / conftest.py / CLAUDE.md edits.
DO NOT merge to BlarAI main yourself. Leave for Co-Lead trusted_scope merge at Phase 3.

WI-8: Post-commit ORACLE verification + completion comment composition. Execute:
cd C:\Users\mrbla\BlarAI git diff main...feature/p5-task11-ea4-test-baseline-drift-investigation --name-only
Expected (sorted, exactly 2 files): docs/ledger/TS_sprint11_ea4_test-baseline-drift.md docs/sprints/sprint_11/test_baseline_drift_investigation.md
Any other path = ORACLE violation -> STOP and report.
Additional Co-Lead-audit verification commands (capture verbatim output for the completion comment): (Get-Content C:\Users\mrbla\BlarAI\docs\sprints\sprint_11\test_baseline_drift_investigation.md | Measure-Object -Line).Lines # Expected: >= 80 (Get-Content C:\Users\mrbla\BlarAI\docs\ledger\TS_sprint11_ea4_test-baseline-drift.md | Measure-Object -Line).Lines # Expected: >= 35 + frontmatter lines Select-String -Path C:\Users\mrbla\BlarAI\docs\sprints\sprint_11\test_baseline_drift_investigation.md -Pattern '^## 1\. Methodology|^## SS1|^### 1\. Methodology' # Expected: >= 1 match (SS1 heading present) Select-String -Path C:\Users\mrbla\BlarAI\docs\sprints\sprint_11\test_baseline_drift_investigation.md -Pattern 'drift_category|Drift category' # Expected: >= 1 match (SS5 conclusion present)
Verify no out-of-scope file appears in the diff. Any out-of-scope file -> ORACLE violation; STOP and report.
Do NOT push or merge. Leave for Co-Lead trusted_scope merge.

ea_prompt.negative_constraints:
DO NOT, under any circumstances:
1. Touch any test file for editing. Every test file is READ-ONLY. Even if WI-4 surfaces a weakened assertion, EA-4 does NOT fix it -- the fix is out of scope; the finding triggers CRITICAL stop-and-escalate per WI-4.
2. Edit pyproject.toml. EA-4 reads it for marker taxonomy; does NOT amend it. Any marker-rename or marker-config recommendation lives in SS6 of the report as advisory text.
3. Edit any conftest.py (root or per-package). READ-ONLY.
4. Edit C:\Users\mrbla\BlarAI\CLAUDE.md. SS6's recommendation is advisory; the actual amendment is a Sprint 12+ follow-up or an EA-2-style Active State refresh.
5. Edit C:\Users\mrbla\BlarAI\docs\TEST_GOVERNANCE.md. SS5/SS6 cite it; no edit.
6. Author or amend any ADR or DEC document. The Sprint 11 DEC-16/17/18 bundle is EA-1's purview (landed). DEC-19 is EA-5's optional purview.
7. Touch any path under C:\Users\mrbla\devplatform\. EA-4 is BlarAI-only writes AND BlarAI-only reads. No devplatform reads are required by the deliverables.
8. Touch any SWAGR / SCR / SDV template (docs/sprints/_templates/). EA-3 already amended these; EA-4 does not.
9. Touch any EA-5 working-set path. No edits to .github/copilot-instructions.md (EA-5), tools/vikunja_mcp/README.md (EA-5), or any devplatform wake template (EA-5).
10. Touch any EA-1, EA-2, or EA-3 working-set path. No edits to docs/ledger/*sprint11_ea1*.md, *sprint11_ea2*.md, *sprint11_ea3*.md; no edits to docs/runbooks/active_state_refresh.md (EA-2); no edits to tools/active_state_refresh.ps1 (EA-2); no edits to docs/sprints/_templates/* (EA-3).
11. Touch active_tasks.yaml, ACTIVE_SPRINT.md, or any wake template (BlarAI or devplatform side).
12. Run pytest with `--collect-only` modifying any cache or coverage data into a committed path. Cache files (`.pytest_cache/`, `.coverage`, etc.) are gitignored; verify they remain so.
13. Push to a remote main, force-push, or bypass Co-Lead trusted_scope merge. The branch lands via the standard feature-branch + Co-Lead merge flow.
14. Bundle downstream work with the comprehension comment. Post comprehension; apply Gate:Pending-SDO; STOP. Wait for SDO Phase 1a verdict before any work beyond required-attachment reads.
15. Silently reconcile inconsistencies. If pytest at won't run (env mismatch, missing dependency), STOP and escalate via the comprehension comment OR shift methodology to (c) marker-resolution-at-HEAD which does not require historical pytest runs. Do NOT skip SS3 / SS4 for lack of historical pytest data -- substitute static analysis at HEAD if needed and document the substitution.
16. Author SS3 / SS4 with placeholder rows. The mature-not-minimal floor requires real test names from real inspection.
17. Continue past SS4 if a fail-closed weakening is detected. The CRITICAL stop-and-escalate is mandatory; SS5/SS6 do not get written, the branch does not get merged.
ea_prompt.mature_not_minimal: Investigation report >= 80 lines of substantive content with SS3 enumerating real test names from real inspection (no `` rows), SS4 producing a real fail-closed-surface table (with verbatim file:line citations and verbatim pre/post assertion-shape captures) OR an empty-with-rationale statement, SS5 conclusion grounded in SS3/SS4 evidence (not hand-waved), SS6 producing a concrete replacement baseline string + concrete cross-reference language. Ledger entry >= 35 lines with verbatim Quality Gate evidence (boundary-hash captures, parent_head capture, Test-Path outputs, HEAD pytest summary line, methodology-output excerpt, fail-closed table summary). Padding to hit floors is rejected -- a focused 95-line investigation grounded in real evidence beats a 150-line report padded with redundant cross-references. The methodology section (SS1) is >= 8 lines explaining the choice and rationale, not a one-line "I picked bisect because it was fastest".
ea_prompt.cross_repo_ordering: EA-4 is BlarAI-only writes AND BlarAI-only reads. There is no cross-repo commit-ordering concern. EA-1 / EA-2 cross-repo commits and EA-3 BlarAI commit are PRE-CONDITIONS that WI-1 verifies. No devplatform interaction occurs in EA-4.
ea_prompt.oracle:
At EA close, after the BlarAI feature-branch commit lands:
cd C:\Users\mrbla\BlarAI git diff main...feature/p5-task11-ea4-test-baseline-drift-investigation --name-only
Expected (sorted, exactly 2 files): docs/ledger/TS_sprint11_ea4_test-baseline-drift.md docs/sprints/sprint_11/test_baseline_drift_investigation.md
Any other path = ORACLE violation. EA captures the diff verbatim in the completion comment on Vikunja task #410.
Additional verification commands for Co-Lead audit: (Get-Content C:\Users\mrbla\BlarAI\docs\sprints\sprint_11\test_baseline_drift_investigation.md | Measure-Object -Line).Lines # Expected: >= 80 Select-String -Path C:\Users\mrbla\BlarAI\docs\sprints\sprint_11\test_baseline_drift_investigation.md -Pattern 'Methodology|methodology' # Expected: >= 1 match (SS1 present) Select-String -Path C:\Users\mrbla\BlarAI\docs\sprints\sprint_11\test_baseline_drift_investigation.md -Pattern 'Fail-closed|fail-closed' # Expected: >= 1 match (SS4 present) Select-String -Path C:\Users\mrbla\BlarAI\docs\sprints\sprint_11\test_baseline_drift_investigation.md -Pattern 'drift category|Drift category|drift_category' # Expected: >= 1 match (SS5 present) Select-String -Path C:\Users\mrbla\BlarAI\docs\sprints\sprint_11\test_baseline_drift_investigation.md -Pattern 'Recommendation|recommendation' # Expected: >= 1 match (SS6 present)
CRITICAL-finding-exit case (fail-closed weakening detected): The report file MAY exist on the feature branch in partial state (SS1-SS4 written, SS5-SS6 omitted or marked PENDING-LA). The ORACLE git-diff --name-only output may still show the same 2 files, but the report length floor (>= 80 lines) is relaxed in the partial-report case (>= 40 lines acceptable with PENDING-LA markers explicit). The branch is NOT merged; the tracking task carries Gate:Pending-Human.
ea_prompt.completion:
After ORACLE passes (or after CRITICAL exit per WI-4), post `[agent:ea_code][phase:completion]` comment on Vikunja tracking task #410 containing: 1. The BlarAI feature-branch commit hash. If Co-Lead trusted_scope merge has already landed (unlikely for EA-4 since the diff is small but doc-only), include the merge commit hash too. 2. The git diff --name-only output verbatim. 3. Line counts: investigation report, ledger entry. 4. The TS timestamp used in the ledger filename. 5. The methodology chosen + one-sentence rationale (SS1 summary). 6. The drift-category determination (benign / suspicious / regression) + one-sentence justification (SS5 summary). 7. The SS4 fail-closed-surface table summary: total rows, count with Weakening = Y, severity distribution if any. 8. The Sprint 12+ recommended baseline string (SS6) verbatim. 9. Confirmation that all WI-1 Test-Path checks returned the expected shape. 10. Verbatim WI-8 line-count and grep outputs verifying SS1/SS4/SS5/SS6 headings present. 11. Confirmation that no negative constraint was violated -- in particular: NO test edits, NO pyproject.toml or conftest.py edit, NO CLAUDE.md edit, NO ADR/DEC writes, NO doctrine template edits, NO devplatform reads or writes, NO touch of EA-1/2/3/5 working-set paths.
Apply label Gate:Pending-SDO (id 9) on task #410. STOP. WAIT for SDO Phase 1b completion-review verdict.
CRITICAL-finding-exit variant: if WI-4 surfaced a fail-closed weakening at HIGH/CRITICAL severity, the completion comment is prefixed `[agent:ea_code][phase:completion] CRITICAL FINDING -- fail-closed assertion weakening detected`, items 6-8 above are replaced by the CRITICAL-finding description (test name, file:line, pre/post assertion shapes, severity), and the label applied is Gate:Pending-Human (label id 11), NOT Gate:Pending-SDO.
Resume the fleet LAST BEFORE posting the completion comment -- ensures the fleet is unpaused for SDO's review-wake to fire cleanly.
ea_prompt.budget_envelope: Effective session cap: 60 min (+ active Budget Lift if granted). TTG: 2 h wall-clock. Trusted_scope merge eligibility: yes (Sprint 11 EA-4 working set is 2 doc files -- the investigation report + the ledger entry; aggregate ~115-200 LOC of new prose; well under DEC-18 thresholds at 3000 LOC / 100 files; all paths under docs/sprints/sprint_11/ + docs/ledger/, which are doctrine surfaces inside the trusted_scope envelope). Methodology execution overhead is the main budget risk -- if a historical pytest run requires environment rehydration that exceeds the session cap, fall back to methodology (c) marker- resolution-at-HEAD which avoids historical runs entirely.
ea_prompt.links: - Continuation: docs/P5_TASK11_SDO_CONTINUATION_v1.0.xml - Sprint 11 SDV (v3): docs/sprints/sprint_11/strategic_design_vision.md - DEC-13 report format: docs/DEC13_REPORT_QUEUE_PROPOSAL_v1.xml - Sprint 8 SWAGR SS8 baseline strings (lower bound) - Sprint 10 SWAGR SS8.1 baseline strings (upper bound) + SS8.1 recommendation for investigation - Sprint 11 EA-1 DEC-17 (Q1-1 ledger format permanence -- this EA's ledger entry follows that schema) - Sprint 11 EA-2 Active State refresh procedure -- SS6 recommendation routes Sprint 12+ baseline-string update via this procedure - CLAUDE.md active state baseline string -- the SS6 target - docs/TEST_GOVERNANCE.md -- marker taxonomy reference
